primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and low hemoglobin (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Progressive AKI is evident with creatinine rising from baseline to 8.9 mg/dL (Stage 3). Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria
Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h with Stage 3 classification
Platelets <100 K/uL meets severe coagulopathy threshold

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order urgent source control evaluation for infection
Start vasopressor support if hypotension develops
Monitor urine output and creatinine trends hourly
Consider platelet transfusion if bleeding risk present
Review excluded creatinine outlier for specimen integrity
doctor_handoff: Confirm infection source, assess fluid resuscitation adequacy, evaluate for bleeding diathesis, and validate AKI staging with renal ultrasound. Review excluded creatinine spike for possible measurement artifact.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
